questions.question: Explain why HSV color space is often preferred over RGB for color-based image segmentation tasks.
expectedPoints: Separation of chromatic (Hue, Saturation) and achromatic (Value) components
More intuitive representation aligned with human color perception
Easier to define color boundaries in Hue-Space plane

questions.question: Compare and contrast three different clustering approaches (k-means, fuzzy c-means, mean shift) for color image segmentation, discussing their advantages, limitations, and appropriate use cases.
expectedPoints: K-means: Requires pre-specified k, simple but sensitive to initialization, good for distinct color clusters
Fuzzy c-means: Allows partial membership, handles color ambiguity better, computationally more expensive
Mean shift: Non-parametric, automatically determines cluster count, better for complex color distributions but computationally intensive
Practical considerations: Application requirements, computational resources, need for automatic cluster determination
Examples of suitable applications for each approach
